message.content:
Search for KubeletConfig examples, documentation, and usage patterns in this Machine Config Operator repository. I need to understand:
1. How to create a KubeletConfig resource targeting worker nodes
2. How to set maxPods and podPidsLimit settings
3. Any relevant YAML examples or test files showing complete KubeletConfig specs
4. What machineConfigPoolSelector looks like

Search in: docs/, examples/, test/, pkg/, vendor/ for KubeletConfig YAML examples and Go structs. Search breadth: medium.

1	# Summary
2	
3	Users need a way to update Kubelet configuration and Kubelet tuneables. Users can do this via MCO currently, but they need to know the correct options to use. We can hopefully help customers by documenting a CRD with the Kubelet tuneables and have the MCO use these tuneables when rendering the kubelet.conf and kubelet.system files to Ignition/disk.
4	
5	Runtime configuration is configured via Kubelet as well. The CRI-O runtime is used by default and is the recommended solution. Some customers may have Docker specific tools or monitoring products they have paid for.
6	
7	# Motivation
8	
9	The MCO contains the logic to write the kubelet.conf configuration file and kubelet.system systemd unit file to Ignition. When Ignition starts on a machine it writes these two files to configure the kubelet. When these resources change within the MachineConfig, the new configs are rewritten and the MachineConfigDaemon is instructed to reboot the machine to pick up the new configs.
10	
11	## Goals
12	
13	The following goals should not require explicit knowledge of a customer to know the right command-line or configuration options to use within the Kubelet.
14	
15	1. changing out of resource handling ([https://docs.openshift.com/container-platform/3.11/admin_guide/out_of_resource_handling.html#out-of-resource-create-config](https://docs.openshift.com/container-platform/3.11/admin_guide/out_of_resource_handling.html#out-of-resource-create-config))
16	
17	2. setting a feature gate ([https://docs.openshift.com/container-platform/3.11/install_config/configuring_ephemeral.html#ephemeral-storage-enabling-ephemeral-storage](https://docs.openshift.com/container-platform/3.11/install_config/configuring_ephemeral.html#ephemeral-storage-enabling-ephemeral-storage))
18	
19	3. setting cpu manager to static ([https://docs.openshift.com/container-platform/3.11/scaling_performance/using_cpu_manager.html](https://docs.openshift.com/container-platform/3.11/scaling_performance/using_cpu_manager.html))
20	
21	4. setting max pods per node ([https://docs.openshift.com/container-platform/3.11/admin_guide/manage_nodes.html#admin-guide-max-pods-per-node](https://docs.openshift.com/container-platform/3.11/admin_guide/manage_nodes.html#admin-guide-max-pods-per-node))
22	
23	5. configuring garbage collection ([https://docs.openshift.com/container-platform/3.11/admin_guide/garbage_collection.html](https://docs.openshift.com/container-platform/3.11/admin_guide/garbage_collection.html))
24	
25	6. configuring node resources ([https://docs.openshift.com/container-platform/3.11/admin_guide/manage_nodes.html#configuring-node-resources](https://docs.openshift.com/container-platform/3.11/admin_guide/manage_nodes.html#configuring-node-resources))
26	
27	## Non-Goals
28	
29	# Proposal
30	
31	Extend the Machine Config Operator to include a KubetletConfig CRD and KubeletConfigController. By using a KubeletConfig CRD there is an implicit allowlist of allowed user controlled options. Upon deleting the KubeletConfig instance the default config is restored.
32	
33	## CRD
34	
35	```
36	apiVersion: apiextensions.k8s.io/v1
37	kind: CustomResourceDefinition
38	metadata:
39	  name: kubeletconfigs.machineconfiguration.openshift.io
40	spec:
41	  group: machineconfiguration.openshift.io
42	  versions:
43	    - name: v1
44	      served: true
45	      storage: true
46	  scope: Cluster
47	  names:
48	    plural: kubeletconfigs
49	    singular: kubeletconfig
50	    kind: KubeletConfig
51	```
52	
53	## SPEC
54	
55	```
56	MachineConfigPoolSelector *metav1.LabelSelector
57	Runtime:
58	  Name  string
59	  Endpoint string
60	KubeletConfig
61	  [KubeletConfigurationSpec](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/release-1.11/pkg/kubelet/apis/kubeletconfig/v1beta1/types.go#L45)
62	```
63	
64	## VALIDATION
65	
66	It's important to note that, since the fields of the kubelet configuration are directly fetched from upstream the validation
67	of those values is handled directly by the kubelet. Please refer to the upstream version of the relavent kubernetes for the
68	valid values of these fields. Invalid values of the kubelet configuration fields may render cluster nodes unusable.
69	
70	## Example - Setting the Kubelet Log Level
71	This is what an example `kubelet config` CR looks like. Note: you must make sure to add a label under `matchLabels` in the KubeletConfig CR:
72	
73	```
74	apiVersion: machineconfiguration.openshift.io/v1
75	kind: KubeletConfig
76	metadata:
77	  name: set-max-pods
78	spec:
79	  logLevel: 5
80	  machineConfigPoolSelector:
81	    matchLabels:
82	      pools.operator.machineconfiguration.openshift.io/worker: ""
83	  kubeletConfig:
84	    maxPods: 100
85	```
86	
87	The logLevel attribute is optional and will default to level 2. Increasing the logLevel
88	uses more IO, CPU, and resources on all the machines within the pool.
89	
90	Save your `kubeletconfig` locally, for example as maxpods.yaml
91	
92	The label in the above example corresponds to the worker MachineConfigPool. By default the master/worker
93	MachineConfigPool has labels pools.operator.machineconfiguration.openshift.io/{worker|master}: "" in OCP 4.6 and later. If you have a custom pool, or have an earlier OCP version, you can instead create a label yourself as follows:
94	
95	```
96	apiVersion: machineconfiguration.openshift.io/v1
97	kind: KubeletConfig
98	metadata:
99	  name: set-max-pods
100	spec:
101	  logLevel: 5
102	  machineConfigPoolSelector:
103	    matchLabels:
104	      custom-kubelet: small-pods
105	  kubeletConfig:
106	    maxPods: 100
107	```
108	
109	To roll out the pods limit changes to all the worker nodes (can switch this to master for the master nodes), add the label that you created, here: `custom-kubelet: small-pods` under labels in the machineConfigPool config:
110	
111	```
112	oc edit machineconfigpool worker
113	```
114	
115	Snippet of the machineConfigPool config with the matching label added:
116	
117	```
118	apiVersion: machineconfiguration.openshift.io/v1
119	kind: MachineConfigPool
120	metadata:
121	  creationTimestamp: 2019-04-10T16:39:39Z
122	  generation: 1
123	  labels:
124	    custom-kubelet: small-pods
125	  name: worker
126	  ...
127	```
128	Now apply the `kubeletconfig` that you created:
129	
130	```
131	$ oc apply -f maxpods.yaml
132	kubeletconfig.machineconfiguration.openshift.io/set-max-pods created
133	```
134	
135	Double check that it was created:
136	
137	```
138	$ oc get kubeletconfig
139	NAME           AGE
140	set-max-pods   6s
141	```
142	
143	Check to ensure that a new 99-worker-XXX-kubelet is created and that a new rendered worker is created:
144	
145	```
146	$ oc get machineconfigs
147	NAME                                 GENERATEDBYCONTROLLER                      IGNITIONVERSION   AGE
148	...
149	99-worker-kubelet-managed            fc45f8b73b2fc61e567f2111181d3e802f2565d7   3.1.0             7s
150	...
151	rendered-worker-45678XYZ             fc45f8b73b2fc61e567f2111181d3e802f2565d7   3.1.0             2s
152	...
153	```
154	
155	The changes should now be rolled out to each node in the worker pool via that new rendered-worker machine config. You can verify by checking
156	that the latest rendered-worker machine-config has been rolled out to the pools successfully:
157	
158	```
159	$ oc get mcp
160	NAME     CONFIG                     UPDATED   UPDATING   DEGRADED   MACHINECOUNT   READYMACHINECOUNT   UPDATEDMACHINECOUNT   DEGRADEDMACHINECOUNT   AGE
161	...
162	worker   rendered-worker-45678XYZ   True      False      False      3              3                   3                     0                      5m
163	...
164	```
165	
166	## Example - Setting the optimal system reserved
167	You can also use `KubeletConfig` to set the optimal system reserved values for memory and cpu.
168	
169	```
170	apiVersion: machineconfiguration.openshift.io/v1
171	kind: KubeletConfig
172	metadata:
173	  name: dynamic-node
174	spec:
175	  autoSizingReserved: true
176	  machineConfigPoolSelector:
177	    matchLabels:
178	      pools.operator.machineconfiguration.openshift.io/worker: ""
179	```
180	
181	The `autoSizingReserved` attribute is optional and will default to the value `false`.
182	
183	Save your `KubeletConfig` locally say, kubelet-dynamic-node.yaml
184	
185	The label in the above example corresponds to the worker MachineConfigPool. Similar approach can be take to apply the `KubeletConfig` to the master or custom pool.
186	
187	## Implementation Details
188	
189	The KubeletConfigController would perform the following steps:
190	
191	1. Validate the user defined KubeletConfig
192	
193	2. Render the current MachineConfig (storage.files.contents[kubelet.conf]) into the KubeletConfiguration structure
194	
195	3. Load the KubeletConfig from the passed in Spec.KubeletConfig
196	
197	4. Use mergo to merge the two structures
198	
199	5. Serialize the KubeletConfig to json
200	
201	6. Create or Update an ignition /etc/kubernetes/kubelet.conf file within a 99-[role]-kubelet-managed MachineConfig
202	
203	After deletion of the KubeletConfig instance the config will be reverted to the original kubelet config.
204	
205	## Runtime Selection
206	
207	### Requirements
208	
209	First time installs will **always** install with CRI-O. After the initial install, the user will be able to switch from CRI-O to Docker, or from Docker to CRI-O if commanded to.
210	
211	Setting the options with the KubeletConfig CRD will cause the node to drain and reboot. Upon reboot, the machine will be reconfigured to use docker as the runtime via Machine Config Daemon and a rollout to each node.
212	
213	**_Note: Docker is only available in Bring-Your-Own-RHEL (User Provisioned Infrastructure) configurations._**
214	
215	TODO: Network Daemon?
216	
217	TODO: crictl configuration?
218	
219	### Runtime Partition Requirements
220	
221	There are specific partitioning schemes that are supported.
222	
223	1. Single Root Partition (/): Single partitioning scheme where everything is on a root partition
224	
225	2. Control Partition (/var): There is a root (/) partition for the filesystem, in addition to a separate /var disk or partition for runtime specific data. This scheme is useful in cases where the customer wants a small root partition or run the runtime via another disk (ie: SSD)
226	
227	3. Control and Log Partition (/var and /var/log): There is a root (/) partition for the filesystem, in addition to a separate /var and /log disk or partition. This scheme is useful in cases where the customer wants to protect from log files filling up the primary image or runtime disk.
228

30	func TestKubeletConfigDefaultUpdateFreq(t *testing.T) {
31		autoSizing := false
32		resources := make(map[string]string)
33		matchLabels := make(map[string]string)
34		matchLabels["pools.operator.machineconfiguration.openshift.io/infra"] = ""

51	func TestKubeletConfigMaxPods(t *testing.T) {
52		autoNodeSizing := true
53	
54		// kc1: systemReservedCgroup: "", enforceNodeAllocatable: [pods]
55		kcRaw1, err := kcfg.EncodeKubeletConfig(&kubeletconfigv1beta1.KubeletConfiguration{
56			MaxPods:                100,
57			SystemReservedCgroup:   "",
58			EnforceNodeAllocatable: []string{kubeletypes.NodeAllocatableEnforcementKey},

64			Spec: mcfgv1.KubeletConfigSpec{
65				AutoSizingReserved: &autoNodeSizing,
66				KubeletConfig: &runtime.RawExtension{

74			MaxPods:                200,
75			SystemReservedCgroup:   "/system.slice",
76			EnforceNodeAllocatable: []string{kubeletypes.NodeAllocatableEnforcementKey, kubeletypes.SystemReservedCompressibleEnforcementKey},
77		}, kubeletconfigv1beta1.SchemeGroupVersion, runtime.ContentTypeJSON)

91			[]string{`"?maxPods"?: (\S+)`, `"?systemReservedCgroup"?: "?([^",\n]*)"?`, `"?enforceNodeAllocatable"?:`},
92			[]string{"maxPods", "systemReservedCgroup", "enforceNodeAllocatable"},

96	
97	// runTestWithKubeletCfg creates a kubelet config and checks whether the expected updates were applied, then deletes the kubelet config and makes
98	// sure the node rolled back as expected
99	// testName is a string to identify the objects created (MCP, MC, kubeletConfig)
100	// regex key is the searching critera in the kubelet.conf. It is expected that a single field is in a capture group, and this field
101	// should equal expectedConfValue upon update
102	// kc1 and kc2 are the kubelet configs to update to and rollback from
103	func runTestWithKubeletCfg(t *testing.T, testName string, regexKey []string, stringKey []string, expectedConfVals [][]string, kc1, kc2 *mcfgv1.KubeletConfig) {
104		cs := framework.NewClientSet("")
105		matchValue := fmt.Sprintf("%s", testName)
106		kcName1 := fmt.Sprintf("kubelet-%s", kc1.GetName())
107		kcName2 := ""
108		if kc2 != nil {
109			kcName2 = fmt.Sprintf("kubelet-%s", kc2.GetName())
110		}
111		poolName := fmt.Sprintf("node-%s", matchValue)
112		mcName := fmt.Sprintf("mc-%s", matchValue)
113	
114		// instead of a bunch of individual defers, we can run through all of them
115		// in a single one
116		cleanupFuncs := make([]func(), 0)
117		defer func() {
118			for _, f := range cleanupFuncs {
119				f()
120			}
121		}()
122	
123		// label one node from the pool to specify which worker to update
124		cleanupFuncs = append(cleanupFuncs, helpers.LabelRandomNodeFromPool(t, cs, "worker", helpers.MCPNameToRole(poolName)))
125		// upon cleaning up, we need to wait for the pool to reconcile after unlabelling
126		cleanupFuncs = append(cleanupFuncs, func() {
127			// the sleep allows the unlabelling to take effect
128			time.Sleep(time.Second * 5)
129			// wait until worker pool updates the node we labelled before we delete the test specific mc and mcp
130			if err := helpers.WaitForPoolComplete(t, cs, "worker", helpers.GetMcName(t, cs, "worker")); err != nil {
131				t.Logf("failed to wait for pool %v", err)
132			}
133		})
134	
135		// cache the old configuration value to check against later
136		node := helpers.GetSingleNodeByRole(t, cs, poolName)
137		// the kubelet.conf format is yaml when in the default state and becomes a json when we apply a kubelet config CR
138		defaultConfVals := []string{}
139		for i, val := range regexKey {
140			if strings.Contains(val, "systemReserved") && stringKey[i] != "systemReservedCgroup" && stringKey[i] != "enforceNodeAllocatable" {
141				defaultConfVals = append(defaultConfVals, "")
142				continue
143			}
144			out, _ := getValueFromKubeletConfig(t, cs, node, val, stringKey[i], kubeletPath)
145			defaultConfVals = append(defaultConfVals, out)
146	
147			// Verify default state for systemReservedCgroup and enforceNodeAllocatable before applying kc1
148			if stringKey[i] == "systemReservedCgroup" {
149				require.True(t, strings.Contains(out, "/system.slice"), "default systemReservedCgroup should be '/system.slice'")
150				t.Logf("Verified default systemReservedCgroup: /system.slice")
151				continue // Skip early exit check for this field since we're testing disable/re-enable
152			}
153			if stringKey[i] == "enforceNodeAllocatable" {
154				require.True(t, strings.Contains(out, "pods"), "default enforceNodeAllocatable should contain 'pods'")
155				require.True(t, strings.Contains(out, "system-reserved-compressible"), "default enforceNodeAllocatable should contain 'system-reserved-compressible'")
156				t.Logf("Verified default enforceNodeAllocatable contains: pods, system-reserved-compressible")
157				continue // Skip early exit check for this field since we're testing disable/re-enable
158			}
159	
160			for _, expect := range expectedConfVals[i] {
161				if defaultConfVals[i] == expect {
162					t.Logf("default configuration value %s same as values being tested against. Consider updating the test", defaultConfVals[i])
163					return
164				}
165			}
166		}
167	
168		// create an MCP to match the node we tagged
169		cleanupFuncs = append(cleanupFuncs, helpers.CreateMCP(t, cs, poolName))
170	
171		// create default mc to have something to verify we successfully rolled back
172		defaultMCConfig := helpers.CreateMC(mcName, poolName)
173		_, err := cs.MachineConfigs().Create(context.TODO(), defaultMCConfig, metav1.CreateOptions{})
174		require.Nil(t, err)
175		cleanupFuncs = append(cleanupFuncs, func() {
176			err := cs.MachineConfigs().Delete(context.TODO(), defaultMCConfig.Name, metav1.DeleteOptions{})
177			require.Nil(t, err, "machine config deletion failed")
178		})
179		defaultTarget := helpers.WaitForConfigAndPoolComplete(t, cs, poolName, defaultMCConfig.Name)
180	
181		// create our first kubelet config and attach it to our created node pool
182		cleanupKcFunc1 := createKcWithConfig(t, cs, kcName1, poolName, &kc1.Spec, "")
183		// wait for the first kubelet config to show up
184		kcMCName1, err := getMCFromKubeletCfg(t, cs, kcName1)
185		require.Nil(t, err, "failed to render machine config from first container runtime config")
186		// ensure the first kubelet config update rolls out to the pool
187		kc1Target := helpers.WaitForConfigAndPoolComplete(t, cs, poolName, kcMCName1)
188		// verify value was changed to match that of the first kubelet config
189		for i, val := range regexKey {
190			out, found := getValueFromKubeletConfig(t, cs, node, val, stringKey[i], kubeletPath)
191			if found {
192				require.Equal(t, expectedConfVals[i][0], out, "value in kubelet config not updated as expected")
193			} else { // sometimes it seems regexp does not work here
194				require.True(t, strings.Contains(out, expectedConfVals[i][0]))
195			}
196			// Additional validation for enforceNodeAllocatable to ensure "pods" is present
197			if stringKey[i] == "enforceNodeAllocatable" {
198				require.True(t, strings.Contains(out, "pods"), "enforceNodeAllocatable should contain 'pods'")
199			}
200		}
201	
202		if kc2 != nil {
203			// create our second kubelet config and attach it to our created node pool
204			cleanupKcFunc2 := createKcWithConfig(t, cs, kcName2, poolName, &kc2.Spec, "1")
205			// wait for the second kubelet config to show up
206			kcMCName2, err := getMCFromKubeletCfg(t, cs, kcName2)
207			require.Nil(t, err, "failed to render machine config from second container runtime config")
208			// ensure the second kubelet config update rolls out to the pool
209			helpers.WaitForConfigAndPoolComplete(t, cs, poolName, kcMCName2)
210			// verify value was changed to match that of the second kubelet config
211			for i, val := range regexKey {
212				out, found := getValueFromKubeletConfig(t, cs, node, val, stringKey[i], kubeletPath)
213				if found {
214					require.Equal(t, out, expectedConfVals[i][1], "value in kubelet config not updated as expected")
215				} else { // sometimes it seems regexp does not work here
216					require.True(t, strings.Contains(out, expectedConfVals[i][1]))
217				}
218				// Additional validation for enforceNodeAllocatable to ensure both "pods" and "system-reserved-compressible" are present
219				if stringKey[i] == "enforceNodeAllocatable" && kc2.Spec.AutoSizingReserved != nil && *kc2.Spec.AutoSizingReserved {
220					require.True(t, strings.Contains(out, "pods"), "enforceNodeAllocatable should contain 'pods'")
221					require.True(t, strings.Contains(out, "system-reserved-compressible"), "enforceNodeAllocatable should contain 'system-reserved-compressible'")
222				}
223			}
224	
225			// cleanup the second kubelet config and make sure it doesn't error
226			err = cleanupKcFunc2()
227			require.Nil(t, err)
228			t.Logf("Deleted KubeletConfig %s", kcName2)
229	
230			// ensure config rolls back to the previous kubelet config as expected
231			helpers.WaitForPoolComplete(t, cs, poolName, kc1Target)
232			// verify that the config value rolled back to that from the first kubelet config
233			for i, val := range regexKey {
234				out, found := getValueFromKubeletConfig(t, cs, node, val, stringKey[i], kubeletPath)
235				if found {
236					require.Equal(t, out, expectedConfVals[i][0], "value in kubelet config not updated as expected")
237				} else { // sometimes it seems regexp does not work here
238					require.True(t, strings.Contains(out, expectedConfVals[i][0]))
239				}
240				// Additional validation for enforceNodeAllocatable to ensure "pods" is present
241				if stringKey[i] == "enforceNodeAllocatable" {
242					require.True(t, strings.Contains(out, "pods"), "enforceNodeAllocatable should contain 'pods'")
243				}
244			}
245	
246			// cleanup the first kubelet config and make sure it doesn't error
247			err = cleanupKcFunc1()
248			require.Nil(t, err)
249			t.Logf("Deleted KubeletConfig %s", kcName1)
250			// ensure config rolls back as expected
251			helpers.WaitForPoolComplete(t, cs, poolName, defaultTarget)
252			// verify that the config value rolled back to the default value
253			for i, val := range regexKey {
254				out, found := getValueFromKubeletConfig(t, cs, node, val, stringKey[i], kubeletPath)
255				// Verify default state has system-reserved-compressible enabled
256				if stringKey[i] == "enforceNodeAllocatable" {
257					require.True(t, strings.Contains(out, "pods"), "enforceNodeAllocatable should contain 'pods' in default state")
258					require.True(t, strings.Contains(out, "system-reserved-compressible"), "enforceNodeAllocatable should contain 'system-reserved-compressible' in default state")
259					continue
260				}
261				if stringKey[i] == "systemReservedCgroup" {
262					require.True(t, strings.Contains(out, "/system.slice"), "systemReservedCgroup should be '/system.slice' in default state")
263					continue
264				}
265				if found {
266					require.Equal(t, out, defaultConfVals[i], "value in kubelet config not updated as expected")
267				} else { // sometimes it seems regexp does not work here
268					require.True(t, strings.Contains(out, defaultConfVals[i]))
269				}
270			}
271		}
272	}
273	
274	// createKcWithConfig takes a config spec and creates a KubeletConfig object
275	// to use this kubelet config, create a pool label key
276	// this function assumes there is a mcp with label 'key='
277	func createKcWithConfig(t *testing.T, cs *framework.ClientSet, name, key string, config *mcfgv1.KubeletConfigSpec, suffix string) func() error {
278		kc := &mcfgv1.KubeletConfig{}
279		kc.ObjectMeta = metav1.ObjectMeta{
280			Name: name,
281		}
282	
283		spec := mcfgv1.KubeletConfigSpec{
284			AutoSizingReserved: config.AutoSizingReserved,
285			MachineConfigPoolSelector: &metav1.LabelSelector{
286				MatchLabels: make(map[string]string),
287			},
288			KubeletConfig: config.KubeletConfig,
289		}
290		spec.MachineConfigPoolSelector.MatchLabels[key] = ""
291		kc.Spec = spec
292		// Add the suffix value the MC created for this kubelet config should have. This helps decide
293		// the priority order
294		if suffix != "" {
295			kc.Annotations = map[string]string{
296				ctrlcommon.MCNameSuffixAnnotationKey: suffix,
297			}
298		}
299	
300		_, err := cs.KubeletConfigs().Create(context.TODO(), kc, metav1.CreateOptions{})
301		require.Nil(t, err)
302		return func() error {
303			return cs.KubeletConfigs().Delete(context.TODO(), name, metav1.DeleteOptions{})
304		}
305	}
306	
307	// getMCFromKubeletCfg returns a rendered machine config that was generated from the kubelet config kcName
308	func getMCFromKubeletCfg(t *testing.T, cs *framework.ClientSet, kcName string) (string, error) {
309		var mcName string
310		// get the machine config created when we deploy the kubelet config
311		if err := wait.PollImmediate(2*time.Second, 5*time.Minute, func() (bool, error) {
312			mcs, err := cs.MachineConfigs().List(context.TODO(), metav1.ListOptions{})
313			if err != nil {
314				return false, err
315			}
316			for _, mc := range mcs.Items {
317				ownerRefs := mc.GetOwnerReferences()
318				for _, ownerRef := range ownerRefs {
319					// TODO can't find anywhere this value is defined publically
320					if ownerRef.Kind == "KubeletConfig" && ownerRef.Name == kcName {
321						mcName = mc.Name
322						return true, nil
323					}
324				}
325			}
326			return false, nil
327		}); err != nil {
328			return "", fmt.Errorf("can't find machine config created by kubelet config %s: %w", kcName, err)
329		}
330		return mcName, nil
331	}
332	
333	// getValueFromKubeletConfig jumps onto the node and gets the kubelet config. It then uses the regexKey to
334	// find the value that is being searched for
335	// regexKey is expected to be in the form `"key": (\S+)` to search for the value of key
336	func getValueFromKubeletConfig(t *testing.T, cs *framework.ClientSet, node corev1.Node, regexKey, stringKey, confPath string) (string, bool) {
337		// get the contents of the kubelet.conf on nodeName
338		out := helpers.ExecCmdOnNode(t, cs, node, "cat", filepath.Join("/rootfs", confPath))
339		t.Log(out)
340	
341		// search based on the regex key. The output should have two members:
342		// one with the entire line `value = key` and one with just the key, in that order
343		re := regexp.MustCompile(regexKey)
344		matches := re.FindStringSubmatch(string(out))
345		if len(matches) != 2 && strings.Contains(string(out), stringKey) {
346			return string(out), false
347		}
348	
349		// If the field is not found in the YAML, it means it's empty/omitted (due to omitempty tags)
350		// This is expected for fields like systemReservedCgroup when set to empty string
351		if len(matches) == 0 {
352			return "", true
353		}
354	
355		require.Len(t, matches, 2, fmt.Sprintf("failed to get %s", regexKey))
356	
357		require.NotEmpty(t, matches[1], "regex %s attempted on kubelet config of node %s came back empty", node.Name, regexKey)
358		return matches[1], true
359	}
360	
361	// TestKubeletAutoSizingDropIn verifies that the auto-sizing script creates a
362	// KubeletConfiguration drop-in with systemReserved values on each node.
363	func TestKubeletAutoSizingDropIn(t *testing.T) {
364		cs := framework.NewClientSet("")
365		dropInPath := "/etc/openshift/kubelet.conf.d/20-auto-sizing.conf"
366	
367		for _, role := range []string{"worker", "master"} {
368			t.Run(role, func(t *testing.T) {
369				node := helpers.GetRandomNode(t, cs, role)
370				dropIn := helpers.ExecCmdOnNode(t, cs, node, "cat", filepath.Join("/rootfs", dropInPath))
371				require.Contains(t, dropIn, "kind: KubeletConfiguration",
372					"%s drop-in should be a KubeletConfiguration", role)
373				require.Contains(t, dropIn, "systemReserved:",
374					"%s drop-in should contain systemReserved", role)
375			})
376		}
377	}
